texto: Later, you told me what your mother had said. How your father, the farmer, rose up slowly. You told me how your mother wailed on the other end of the phone, grieving her loss and complaining about the basketball of a goitre perched on her shoulder. She told you, your father walked onto the veranda and saw a chook floating ten feet above the ground. The chook didn’t flap a feather and just sat there brooding, swaying in the breeze.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jon Gresham sobre temas ligados a familia. A afirmação “Later, you told me what your mother had said. How your father, the farmer, rose up slowly. You to…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, belonging, booksactually, útil em momentos de luto e termino.

frase_original: Later, you told me what your mother had said. How your father, the farmer, rose up slowly. You told me how your mother wailed on the other end of the phone, grieving her loss and complaining about the basketball of a goitre perched on her shoulder. She told you, your father walked onto the veranda and saw a chook floating ten feet above the ground. The chook didn’t flap a feather and just sat there brooding, swaying in the breeze.

texto: We weren’t happy together but we lived in a state of easy, mild contentment. We shared everything except the stupid fucking secret hanging round your neck. I imagined tiny photographs: portraits in sepia of your parents, their faces partially obscured by goitres. Meanwhile, maybe not tomorrow, maybe not next year, maybe not even in a decade from now but one day: the planet would fall apart.
explicacao: Citação de Jon Gresham sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “We weren’t happy together but we lived in a state of easy, mild contentment. We shared everything…” articula felicidade, booksactually, future em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e future.
